hawkish_phrases: banks expected a net tightening of credit standards on loans to firms in the third quarter of 2020
credit standards for loans to households had tightened in the second quarter
risks of a persistent increase in the unemployment rate
non-performing loans and corporate insolvencies as a result of the pandemic could affect bank balance sheets
the balance of risks to the euro area growth outlook to remain on the downside
dovish_phrases: inflation pressures were expected to remain very subdued on account of the sharp decline in real GDP
weaker demand would put downward pressure on inflation
market-based indicators of longer-term inflation expectations remained at very depressed levels
the outlook for inflation remained subdued, underscoring the view that there was no room for complacency
ample monetary policy stimulus would remain necessary to support the economic recovery
summary: The Governing Council held rates unchanged (main refinancing at 0.00%, deposit at -0.50%) and maintained the full PEPP envelope of €1,350 billion with flexible implementation through at least June 2021, with a mildly dovish bias emphasizing subdued inflation pressures, very depressed inflation expectations, and the necessity of ample monetary stimulus despite early signs of recovery.
